title: Build clearance tracker for Home Depot and Lowe's

requirements.text: Create a backend data model to store clearance items with fields: store name (Home Depot or Lowe's), product name, original price, clearance price, discount percentage, product category, store location (address, city, state, zip code, coordinates), availability status, and timestamp of last update
acceptanceCriteria: Data model supports both Home Depot and Lowe's items
Price fields allow decimal values
Location data includes coordinates for distance calculations
Timestamp tracks when item data was last updated

requirements.text: Implement backend functions to add, update, retrieve, and delete clearance items with filtering capabilities by store name, location, and price range
acceptanceCriteria: Can add new clearance items with all required fields
Can filter items by store (Home Depot or Lowe's)
Can filter items by proximity to a given location
Can filter items by price range
Can update existing items and delete old entries

requirements.text: Create a main page that displays all clearance items from both Home Depot and Lowe's in a grid or list view, showing product name, store name, original price, clearance price, discount percentage, and store location
acceptanceCriteria: Items from both stores are displayed together
Each item card shows all key information clearly
Store name is visually distinguishable (Home Depot vs Lowe's)
Discount percentage is prominently displayed
Layout is responsive and works on mobile devices

requirements.text: Implement location-based filtering that allows users to see only clearance items from stores near their location, with the ability to specify a radius (e.g., within 10, 25, or 50 miles)
acceptanceCriteria: User can input their location (zip code or city)
User can select a distance radius from preset options
Items are filtered to show only those within the specified radius
Distance from user's location is displayed for each item
Clear indication when no items are found within the radius

requirements.text: Add filter controls to allow users to filter items by store (Home Depot only, Lowe's only, or both), product category, and price range with min/max inputs
acceptanceCriteria: Store filter toggles between Home Depot, Lowe's, or both
Category filter includes common categories (tools, appliances, building materials, etc.)
Price range filter has min and max input fields
Filters can be combined and applied together
Clear or reset button to remove all filters

requirements.text: Display a deals section that highlights the best discounts (highest percentage off) from both stores, sorted by discount percentage in descending order
acceptanceCriteria: Best deals are shown in a prominent section
Items are sorted by discount percentage (highest first)
At least top 10-20 deals are displayed
Section is visually distinct from the main item list
Each deal shows the discount percentage prominently

requirements.text: Create a clean, modern visual design with a cohesive color scheme that uses warm, approachable tones like amber and terracotta, contemporary sans-serif typography, and card-based layouts with subtle shadows and rounded corners for an inviting shopping experience
acceptanceCriteria: Color palette uses warm tones consistently throughout the app
Typography is clear and readable with appropriate hierarchy
Card layouts have consistent spacing and visual treatment
Design feels modern and approachable
Store branding (Home Depot/Lowe's) is respected but integrated into the cohesive design

constraints: Must support clearance items from both Home Depot and Lowe's stores
Location filtering must work with user-provided location data
All prices must support decimal values for accurate pricing
nonGoals: Direct integration with Home Depot or Lowe's APIs
